title: symphyotrichum dumosum var. strictior
intro: Symphyotrichum dumosum var. strictior, also known as New England aster or stiff white aster, is a type of flowering plant in the sunflower family, characterized by its white or pinkish flowers and compact growth habit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and essential oils. Consuming the plant or using its herbal preparations may help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and digestive issues, and it has also been used to treat wounds and skin irritations. The plant can be prepared in various ways, including making teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
